sourceMovie.title: Unleashed
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Unleashed for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Louis Leterrier keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Unleashed clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kick-Ass
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Unleashed clicked for you, Kick-Ass (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Unleashed?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 2, Kick-Ass, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum—each shares DNA with Unleashed while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Unleashed?
faq.answer: Unleashed (2005) is best described as Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Unleashed worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
